text: The surgical setup includes six trochars: a 12 mm port for the camera, two 8 mm ports for the robotic arms, two ports (12 mm and 5 mm) for stapling and assistance, and an additional 5 mm port for liver protection.
The surgeon performs adhesiolysis on the left lobe of the liver, a small gastric pouch, alimentary limb, and gastro-jejunostomy using robotic scissors, carefully separating adhesions in these areas.
The surgeon performs a block resection of the anastomosis, revealing a complex gastrojejunal gastric fistula.
The surgeon performs adhesiolysis on the proximal gastric bulge, carefully separating adhesions using robotic instruments.
The surgeon uses a stapling device to resize the gastric pouch, adjusting its dimensions within the surgical field.
The surgeon sutures the fistula on the excluded stomach using a contiguous barbell suture, with the surgical field showing the excluded stomach and the suture technique being applied.
The surgeon performs an epiplasty, using tissue to cover and reinforce the area to prevent recurrence of the fistula.
The surgeon uses a stapling device to secure the proximal alimentary limb, employing a three-staple technique to ensure closure.

The surgeon performs a new gastrojejunal anastomosis using Hansen's technique, employing a running absorbable barrel suture that is cut without a knot, creating a seamless connection between the stomach and jejunum.
The surgeon injects methylene blue stained saline through an orogastric tube to test for asthmatic leakage, observing the flow of the dye to identify any leaks.
The surgeon applies ferritin sealant to the surgical site, completing the procedure without inserting a drain.
